summary: Iteration 9 — verified real Resend integration for password reset. Backend: 5/5 pytest tests pass; no reset_token/reset_link/mocked field in any response; admin@quizdantan.fr returns email_sent=false (Resend test-mode restriction, expected); nadine.zebdi@gmail.com returns email_sent=true with real Resend id logged (e.g. id=43eca210-...). Unknown email returns generic message with no enumeration. E2E flow (register → forgot → token-from-logs → reset → login-with-new-pwd) works; old password rejected; token cannot be reused. Frontend: /forgot-password shows clean form with data-testid forgot-email & forgot-submit, after submit shows forgot-sent block with 'Vérifiez votre boîte mail' heading. NO mock UI elements (forgot-reset-link, forgot-copy, forgot-use-link) present anywhere. forgot-retry resets the form correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py — for UNKNOWN email path, the response omits 'email_sent' key, but for KNOWN email it adds 'email_sent': bool. This is a minor enumeration vector (presence/absence of the key reveals whether the email exists). Consider always returning 'email_sent': false for unknown emails to make responses indistinguishable.
server.py — _send_reset_email logs the full reset_link to backend logs (line 349 [RESET] ...). Useful for testing but in production this is sensitive; consider gating behind a DEBUG flag.
server.py is approaching 970 lines — consider splitting into modules (auth, quiz, payments, challenges, promo) for maintainability.
test_credentials: admin@quizdantan.fr / Admin2026! ; nadine.zebdi@gmail.com / test1234 (Resend account email)
seed_data_creation: test_forgot_password.py creates TEST_reset_<ts>@example.com users for E2E flow (not cleaned up but harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Real Resend integration verified. Forgot-password API never leaks token/link/mocked fields. Frontend mock UI completely removed. Backend logs the reset link as '[RESET] <email> -> <FRONTEND_URL>/reset-password?token=<token>' — useful for E2E testing. Resend test mode only delivers to the account owner (nadine.zebdi@gmail.com); other addresses return email_sent=false which is expected. Minor enumeration note: omitting 'email_sent' key on unknown emails reveals existence (low risk, documented above).
